description: Evaluate the remaining first-run configuration need after Nova’s help, tutor, config UI, and sparse TOML defaults. Add a dedicated yzx onboard flow only if those surfaces do not provide a clear successful first setup. Any accepted flow writes only Nova-owned TOML through the canonical config owner and never ports Classic prompts or sidecars. Prepared destination for closed Classic bead yazelix-rqj and open GitHub issue #445; external_ref transfers only during the atomic source swap.
acceptance_criteria: Test the fresh-user path through bare yzx help, tutor, config UI, launch, and sparse config. Identify a concrete failure before adding another command. If onboarding is accepted, define the minimal prompts/output, interruption and overwrite behavior, and canonical TOML write path; otherwise close issue #445 with evidence that existing surfaces satisfy it.

title: Start the Herdr-inspired Nova session attach flow
description: Use Zellij’s existing named-session primitives so canonical yzx can reopen or attach to the intended managed Nova session without breaking Yazi-to-Helix bridge identity. Make a fresh independent session explicit. The canonical executable is `yzx`. Do not add a Yazelix daemon, PTY ownership, file-descriptor handoff, or a session-management framework. This is the first practical slice after the completed Herdr research.
acceptance_criteria: Define plain launch/enter, attach, and explicit fresh-session semantics before implementation. Apply the managed layout only when creating a session, derive bridge identity from durable session state, and preserve current Mars/Zellij/Yazi/Helix behavior. Focused checks prove command assembly and identity; manual dogfooding covers create, detach, reattach, Yazi-to-existing-Helix open, and explicit fresh session.

title: Reassess Yazi materializer extraction with a second consumer
description:
## Why

The bounded Yazi materializer may eventually serve main Yazelix or another runtime, but extracting it before a second consumer would add release, pinning, API, and coordination overhead without deleting any real owner. Reassess only after the local crate's contract is stable and another consumer is ready to adopt it.

## Evidence

- `ARCHITECTURE.md:123-142`: separate repositories require independent users, release cadence, a stable artifact/API, low product coupling, and deletion of a real local owner.
- The local materializer implementation bead establishes a product-coupled config-home contract first.
- Main Yazelix currently owns a broader independent Yazi materialization surface; theoretical reuse is not adoption.

## Decision Criteria

Extract only if all are true:

- A concrete second consumer will call the same input/output contract.
- The second consumer deletes or relinquishes its existing materialization owner.
- The CLI/library contract is stable enough to version independently.
- Runtime-specific paths and policy can be provided without compatibility adapters or duplicate owners.
- Cross-repo release/pin updates are cheaper than atomic local changes.

Keep local if any are true:

- Yazelix Nova remains the only caller.
- Main Yazelix still requires broader import/migration behavior.
- Extraction needs Nova-specific path shims.
- Changes still need to land atomically with `yzx-yazi`.

## Scope

In scope when reactivated:
- Inspect all current callers and the local crate API/CLI.
- Identify the concrete second consumer and the owner it will delete.
- Compare local-crate versus shared-repo release costs.
- Decide keep local or extract.
- If extracting, create implementation beads with exact repository, artifact, API, and migration boundaries.
- Update ARCHITECTURE with the decision.

Out of scope:
- Do not extract code during this decision bead.
- Do not create a generic Yazi configuration framework.
- Do not add compatibility adapters for hypothetical consumers.
- Do not make extraction block power-user functionality.

## Verification

- `rg -n "yzx-yazi-config|yzx-yazi" .` and equivalent searches in the proposed consumer should identify every real caller.
- If no second consumer exists, close with a keep-local decision and no code changes.
- If extraction is selected, the follow-up plan must name the deleted owner and independent verification commands.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop immediately if no concrete second consumer exists.
- Stop if the proposed consumer cannot delete its current owner.
- Stop if the API remains coupled to Yazelix Nova paths or atomic runtime changes.

## Maintenance Notes

Effort: S decision. Risk: LOW. Confidence: HIGH.

Current recommendation: keep the crate local. Rejected: extraction based only on possible future reuse.
notes: The bounded power-user Yazi outcome is complete. This remains deferred as an independent architecture decision and should reactivate only when a concrete second consumer can delete an existing owner.

title: Design image-backed Yazelix visual surfaces
description: Decide which image-backed experiences belong in Yazelix after Nova: terminal-safe screen styles, curated website/demo media, or explicitly terminal-owned backgrounds. Keep terminal transparency/background ownership in Mars or the host terminal and keep large media out of the Nova runtime unless a chosen feature requires it.
acceptance_criteria: Record the desired user outcomes, compare terminal-safe screen rendering, website/demo media, and terminal-native backgrounds, and assign each accepted outcome to one owner. Any runtime asset choice includes size, license, cache, platform, and garbage-collection impact. Create implementation beads only for maintainer-approved surfaces.

title: Launch the Yazelix website at yazelix.com
description: Publish a thin docs-first Yazelix website that explains the product, provides current Nova installation and configuration entrypoints, and shows the workspace clearly. The website repository owns implementation; canonical Yazelix owns the public product issue and source-of-truth links. Preserve useful current site evidence, but discard the Classic site-audit child explosion and stale JSONC/version copy.
acceptance_criteria: yazelix.com is published with accurate Nova install, configure, update/recovery, keybinding, and project entrypoints; obsolete Classic and temporary staging identity is absent. The site builds and its core routes/outbound links pass. Large media and site-specific planning remain in the website repository.
notes: Transferred implementation evidence: the private local site repository at /home/lucca/pjs/personal/yazelix-site has an Astro + Starlight + MDX + Pagefind implementation at commit cb4cc24, with product, start, configure, update, recover, keybindings, reference, and blog routes. At the Classic snapshot, bun run check, bun run build, and 22 Playwright desktop/mobile checks passed. Treat that as a starting artifact only: replace Classic version/config/path copy with canonical Nova truth before publication, and keep future site-specific tasks in the website repository rather than recreating the discarded Classic audit subtree.

title: Expand bounded live settings apply coverage
description: Audit which Nova config fields can apply safely to the current session through their native owners and which must remain next-session settings. Add live apply only where semantics, failure reporting, and ownership are clear. Do not create a general watcher, duplicate Ratconfig, or promise live reload for package/session-scoped changes.
acceptance_criteria: Classify every candidate setting as safely live, next-session, package-time, or rejected with its owner and reason. Implement only selected high-value live fields through existing native owners, report failures visibly, and verify the current and next session agree. No polling watcher or duplicate config representation.

title: Evaluate bounded host-tool sourcing after the Nova beta
description:
## Why

GitHub issue #584 was a concrete user request to omit selected bundled leaf tools, use host-managed versions, reduce duplicate Nix closure size, and avoid Yazelix shadowing the user’s preferred toolchain. Classic implemented that request through #585 with `runtime_tool_sources`, `lib.mkYazelix`, a package builder, runtime-tool registry/manifest, and doctor policy. Nova intentionally does not carry that architecture, but the underlying user problem deserves a fresh evaluation after the canonical beta rather than being forgotten.

This is a Nova-native product decision. It does not promise to restore the Classic API or implementation.

## Irreducible User Outcomes

Evaluate these separately:

1. **Command preference:** selected integrations invoke the host-managed command instead of Nova’s packaged binary.
2. **Closure reduction:** the selected package and its unique dependencies are absent from the realized Nova closure.
3. **Failure clarity:** missing or incompatible host tools fail visibly and actionably.
4. **Reproducibility:** the default package remains complete and deterministic; opting out is explicit.

A solution that only changes PATH precedence does not satisfy closure reduction. A solution that only omits packages without defining command/failure behavior does not satisfy host-tool sourcing.

## Evidence

- https://github.com/luccahuguet/yazelix/issues/584 records the user request and a brittle placeholder-package workaround.
- https://github.com/luccahuguet/yazelix/issues/585 records the broader Classic implementation.
- The requester explicitly valued the shipped result and intended to try it.
- Main decision `yazelix-slim-runtime-boundaries-7ej8l.5.3` removes the Classic builder/registry surface from Nova v1 and requires this loss to be explicit.
- `the completed staging work` freezes the slim v1 Nix/Home Manager API and must not absorb this evaluation before beta.
- Current Nova packaging embeds direct store paths and one complete runtime package, so Classic host-source modes cannot be copied mechanically.

## Timing

Run after `yazelix-8u5-3-j8h` publishes the canonical Nova beta. Use actual beta closure measurements, package references, user feedback, and the final `yzx run`/doctor/environment behavior. This bead does not block beta or stable v1 by default. If the evaluation discovers a P0/P1 adoption blocker, explicitly revise the stabilization graph rather than silently expanding this bead.

## Options To Compare

- Keep the fixed complete package and whole-package Home Manager override only.
- Prefer host commands for a very small allowlist while accepting no closure savings.
- Add bounded build-time omission for a very small allowlist of leaf tools, with one Nix owner and one generated runtime fact source.
- Split genuinely optional integrations into independently installed packages without a general tool registry.
- Publish a documented custom-package recipe outside the supported module/API.
- Reintroduce a generalized Classic-style builder/registry. Treat this as the high-cost baseline to beat, not the default.

Candidate tools must be leaf integrations, not bootstrap/runtime owners. Mars, Zellij, Nushell, graphics wrappers, core utilities, and package-owned helpers are excluded unless a separate architecture decision proves otherwise.

## Questions

- Which tools dominate unique Nova closure size after shared dependencies are accounted for?
- Which packaged tools currently shadow real user-managed commands?
- Can one small allowlist solve the demonstrated use cases without a generic registry?
- Can doctor/runtime consume one generated fact source without duplicating Nix policy in Rust?
- Does Home Manager need a first-class option, or is a whole-package/custom-package path sufficient?
- How does the design behave on Darwin as well as Linux?
- How many beta users actually request this after seeing Nova’s package size and behavior?

## Acceptance Criteria

- Measure the complete Nova package closure and unique marginal closure for plausible leaf candidates on at least the primary Linux system; evaluate Darwin constraints even if exact closure measurement is unavailable.
- Re-read #584/#585 and collect any Nova beta feedback rather than treating Classic architecture as the requirement.
- Compare the options above against LOC/ownership, Nix evaluation cost, closure savings, command precedence, doctor behavior, platform support, and failure modes.
- Record one decision: keep fixed package, document a custom-package escape hatch, accept one bounded host-source slice, or defer with a concrete evidence threshold.
- If accepted, define an exact tool allowlist, one source of truth, Home Manager/flake shape, diagnostics, package impact, focused verification, and create a separate implementation bead.
- If rejected, explain the user-facing alternative honestly and state what future evidence would reopen the decision.
- Do not change source, public API, or stable-release dependencies in this decision bead.

## Dependency Decision

No crates or package changes belong in this evaluation. A future implementation must record production/dev crates, in-house logic, and packaging impact separately. Default bias: Nix/std and one generated fact source; reject frameworks and duplicate registries.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the work starts copying Classic `runtime_tool_sources`, `mkYazelix`, manifests, or validators before proving they are necessary.
- Stop if a proposal claims closure savings without measuring unique retained references.
- Stop if host preference can silently select an incompatible tool.
- Stop if beta/stable scope expands without explicit maintainer approval.

Effort: M decision. Risk: MED/HIGH because package composition and PATH semantics affect reliability. Confidence: HIGH that evaluation is warranted; intentionally undecided on implementation.

title: Validate mobile SSH access to Nova sessions
description: Validate the smallest supported way to access canonical Nova from an existing SSH-capable Android or mobile terminal and attach to a managed Zellij session. Build on Nova’s headless enter and session work; do not create an Android app or mobile package unless validation proves a concrete unmet need and the user approves it.
acceptance_criteria: A published Nova package is exercised from a representative mobile SSH client against a host session. Documentation covers yzx enter or session attach, terminal capability requirements, authentication/security caveats, and known input/display limitations. Any failure is classified to Nova, Zellij, terminal, or docs; no mobile app is added by default.

title: Evaluate Yazi themes and flavors in Nova
description: Determine whether Nova's managed native Yazi files already provide a clear theme and flavor workflow and identify the smallest remaining user-visible gap. Preserve native Yazi ownership through theme.toml, package.toml, Ratconfig file actions, and Home Manager text/source passthroughs. Do not add a second theme registry or broad materializer.
acceptance_criteria: Test installation and activation of a representative Yazi flavor with writable and Home Manager-owned Nova config. Document the exact workflow and ownership boundary. Close as already supported if current surfaces are sufficient; otherwise create one focused implementation bead for the demonstrated gap.

title: Design Nix-free installer and release bundle
description:
Goal: offer a Nix-free Yazelix install path after Yazelix Nova is better shaped, without replacing Nix as the build/release system.

User-visible contract:
A user can install Yazelix with a curl-style installer, get a `yzx` command in their PATH, and run `yzx help`, `yzx enter`, and `yzx launch` without installing Nix. Nix remains the deterministic build/release factory that produces the bundle.

Recommended shape:
- Build release bundles with Nix.
- Installer detects OS/arch, downloads one prebuilt bundle, verifies it, extracts it under `~/.local/share/yazelix/versions/<version>`, updates `current`, and symlinks `~/.local/bin/yzx`.
- Bundle contains the runtime tools/assets: Mars, Yazelix Zellij fork, Helix fork, Yazi, Nushell, lazygit, starship, carapace, zoxide, plugins/WASMs/configs/assets.
- Add update/uninstall behavior only when the initial install path is stable.

Do not start this before the runtime shape is stable enough:
- canonical command naming is settled (`yzx`)
- Mars/macOS has had contributor validation
- config surfaces are mostly settled
- popups/bar/tutor/keymaps are no longer moving quickly

First implementation slice when ready:
Prove the runtime can be relocatable by resolving binaries/configs relative to a bundle root instead of relying on embedded `/nix/store` paths. Keep this as a contract before writing the public installer script.

Rejected for now:
- Installing Nix from the installer: defeats the purpose of reducing adoption friction.
- Flatpak first: sandboxing and terminal/filesystem/agent behavior make it a worse first distribution target.
- One static binary: unrealistic because Yazelix is a multi-tool runtime bundle.
notes: Prime-beads review 2026-06-27: keep this as future release/distribution planning, not ready implementation work. Reactivate after command naming/swap strategy, Mars/macOS validation, config surfaces, popups, tutor, and keymap surfaces are stable enough to make a relocatable bundle contract meaningful.

title: Build a Nova-native agent command center
description: Give Yazelix a local-first mission-control surface for starting, observing, focusing, reviewing, validating, and archiving multiple agent task lanes. Reuse Nova Zellij sessions, worktrees, Beads, and existing popups. Begin passive-first; do not add a Yazelix daemon, arbitrary send-input fleet control, or provider-framework sprawl.
acceptance_criteria: A written first-slice contract defines task-lane identity, durable state, session/worktree ownership, and passive status derivation. One small vertical slice can start or register a lane, list it, and focus or inspect its work without disturbing unrelated sessions. Validation covers stale-lane handling and worktree/session safety. Broader automation remains explicitly deferred.

title: Reassess yzx-open extraction only with a second consumer
description:
## Why

`crates/yzx-open` is the only local helper that might eventually deserve extraction, because it implements a concrete editor-open/Helix-bridge protocol. Today it is still tightly coupled to Yazelix Nova state, Zellij pane behavior, and the managed Helix wrapper, so extracting it now would add release/API overhead without a second consumer. The decision should be revisited only when main Yazelix, another Yazelix runtime, or a non-Yazelix tool wants to consume the same open protocol.

## Evidence

- `crates/yzx-open/src/main.rs:17`: `Config` is built from Yazelix-specific environment such as editor, Zellij binary, state dir, session id, Zellij session name, and log level.
- `crates/yzx-open/src/main.rs:99`: `Config::from_env` reads `YAZELIX_STATE_DIR`, `YAZELIX_HELIX_BRIDGE_SESSION_ID`, `YZX_EDITOR`, `YZX_ZELLIJ`, and Zellij session state.
- `crates/yzx-open/src/main.rs:119`: bridge discovery assumes the Yazelix Helix bridge directory under `state_dir/helix_bridge/<session_id>`.
- `crates/yzx-open/src/main.rs:255`: bridge requests use a protocol that is currently owned by Yazelix Helix integration.
- `crates/yzx-open/src/main.rs:299`: request payloads are Helix-specific actions: `helix.open_directory` and `helix.open_files`.
- `crates/yzx-open/src/main.rs:321`: fallback behavior shells out to Zellij to open a managed editor pane.
- `flake.nix:229`: the helper is packaged directly from `./crates/yzx-open` into the Yazelix Nova runtime.
- `flake.nix:258`: the Yazi wrapper injects `YZX_OPEN`, `YZX_ZELLIJ`, and managed editor variables for this helper.

## Decision To Revisit

Reassess whether `yzx-open` should become a separate repo, shared crate, or remain local only when at least one concrete second consumer exists.

Criteria that would justify extraction:
- Another repo needs to call the same bridge/open protocol.
- The bridge protocol has stable versioning and compatibility expectations.
- The helper can expose a small documented API/CLI without depending on Yazelix Nova packaging internals.
- Release cadence can be independent without making Yazelix Nova harder to change.

Criteria that keep it local:
- The only caller is Yazelix Nova's packaged Yazi/sidebar flow.
- The fallback behavior remains Zellij-pane-specific.
- Protocol changes need to land atomically with Yazelix Helix and the `yzx` runtime.
- Extraction would require compatibility shims or broad environment adapters.

## Scope

In scope when this decision is revisited:
- Compare current `crates/yzx-open` callers in this repo and main Yazelix.
- Identify whether a stable bridge/open API exists or whether this is still product glue.
- Decide between local crate, shared crate within this repo, separate repo, or no extraction.
- Record the decision in ARCHITECTURE and close this bead with the chosen path.

Out of scope:
- Do not extract `yzx-open` now.
- Do not create a compatibility layer for hypothetical callers.
- Do not rename the CLI or change bridge semantics as part of this decision.
- Do not make this a blocker for the canonical swap unless another bead explicitly elevates it.

## Verification

- If the decision is to keep local, `br close` this bead with notes naming the checked callers and why extraction is still not justified.
- If the decision is to extract, create implementation beads with exact repo/crate/API scope before moving code.
- `cargo test --manifest-path crates/yzx-open/Cargo.toml` should exit 0 for any local refactor done during investigation.
- `nix flake check` should exit 0 if package wiring is touched during investigation.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if no second consumer exists; keep this deferred.
- Stop if extraction would require support for non-Zellij fallback behavior without an explicit user-chosen product requirement.
- Stop if protocol ownership between Yazelix Nova and Yazelix Helix is unclear; resolve that decision before moving code.

## Maintenance Notes

Current recommendation: keep `yzx-open` local. Separate repo extraction requires independent users, independent release cadence, and a stable API, not just a file that could be reused someday.

title: Make the native bottom status bar optional
description: Allow users to reclaim the native one-row Zellij hint/status surface while preserving discoverable mode help through the smallest viable native or on-demand path. Work from Nova’s current layouts; do not port Classic bar materialization or create a second permanent status system. Merge the useful outcome of GitHub issue #621 here.
acceptance_criteria: A decision or implementation proves whether Nova can omit or toggle the native bottom row without breaking modes, layouts, popups, narrow widths, or help discoverability. If accepted, the row is genuinely reclaimed and the replacement help path is documented. If rejected, record the Zellij limitation and close without a second status implementation.

title: Make Nova bar widgets degrade progressively under tab pressure
description: When long or numerous tabs consume width, hide lower-priority right-side widgets progressively instead of dropping the complete right area. The bar child owns rendering and Nova owns the published child revision plus integration smoke. Use Nova’s fixed widget order; do not port Classic widget-tray configuration.
acceptance_criteria: Reproduce whole-right-side loss on published Nova. Define widget priority from the fixed Nova render request, implement the smallest child-owned progressive degradation supported by zjstatus, preserve native tab behavior, publish the child, update the Nova lock, and manually verify long-name/many-tab layouts. Stop and record the smallest alternative if zjstatus cannot express it cleanly.

title: Design restrained Nova notifications
description: Define a small event and delivery policy for action-required, failure, and long-running completion signals without creating a noisy status system. Prefer existing terminal/Zellij surfaces; any desktop delivery must be explicitly platform-gated and optional. Integrate agent-lane events with the command-center owner rather than duplicating agent state.
acceptance_criteria: Define the first event set, urgency, suppression/deduplication, delivery owner, platform behavior, and user control before implementation. One accepted vertical slice demonstrates a valuable notification without noise and has focused routing/suppression tests. Desktop helpers are gated; agent state remains owned by the command center.

title: Evaluate shared build ownership for first-party Zellij WASI plugins
description: After Nova v1, compare the two child-owned WASI build recipes with a neutral published shared builder. Extract only if total owner count, LOC, inputs, release steps, and failure boundaries shrink and both child recipes are deleted. Nova remains a package consumer and contract validator.
acceptance_criteria: Record current and proposed LOC, owners, inputs, releases, platform gates, and failure boundaries. Extraction wins only if both plugin children consume one published neutral builder and delete their duplicate generic recipes while Linux and Darwin package contracts pass. Otherwise close with accepted-duplication evidence. Nova gains no build implementation.

title: Add sidebar-aware columns layout mode
description:
## Why

With a single managed Yazi sidebar, Yazelix Nova can bring back a useful vertical split shape without reintroducing main Yazelix's broader layout system. The intended model is not multiple real startup layouts; it is one startup layout plus sidebar-aware swap variants for the main content area.

## Evidence

- `layout.kdl:13`: the real startup layout has one vertical split containing the managed `sidebar` pane.
- `layout.swap.kdl:1`: the current open variant puts all non-sidebar panes in one stacked content area.
- `layout.swap.kdl:13`: the current closed variant only collapses the sidebar; there is no alternate content arrangement.
- `config.kdl:120`: sidebar toggle currently uses raw `NextSwapLayout`, which must be fixed before adding more variants.
- `/home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-zellij-pane-orchestrator/src/layout_state_contract.rs:20`: the shared pane orchestrator currently models only one layout family, `Single`.
- `/home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-zellij-pane-orchestrator/src/layout_state_contract.rs:44`: the orchestrator's swap order only knows `single_*` and agent variants, so it cannot preserve sidebar state across a new columns mode yet.

## User-Visible Behavior

A user can switch the main work area between the current stacked mode and a columns mode while keeping exactly one managed Yazi sidebar. Toggling the sidebar preserves the selected content mode: stacked-open -> stacked-closed, columns-open -> columns-closed, and back. The startup session remains the current open stacked/sidebar shape.

## Scope

In scope:
- Add a sidebar-aware columns content mode to Yazelix Nova's swap layout contract.
- Keep one real `layout.kdl`; do not add alternate startup layout files.
- Add open/closed swap variants for the columns mode in `layout.swap.kdl`.
- Preserve the current stacked behavior as the default startup mode.
- Update layout checks to prove every variant contains exactly one managed `sidebar` pane and that open/closed widths are coherent.
- Update the pane-orchestrator child package only as much as needed to understand content mode plus sidebar state.
- Update the Nova flake lock/input after the child package change, if the child package is touched.
- Add a semantic command/key path for cycling content mode only if the product key is chosen in the implementation slice; do not overload `Alt Shift h`.
- Update README, ARCHITECTURE, CHANGELOG, and LOC scorecard for user-visible layout/key behavior.

Out of scope:
- Do not port main Yazelix layout families wholesale.
- Do not add a bottom-terminal layout in this bead.
- Do not add right-sidebar/agent layout variants as part of this columns slice.
- Do not make raw Zellij swap cycling the user-facing layout control.
- Do not make layout KDL user-configurable.
- Do not add a general layout DSL or separate layout crate.

## Implementation Notes

1. Land or depend on `the completed staging work` first so `Alt Shift h` remains a sidebar toggle after more swap variants exist.
2. Prefer low-churn naming if the shared orchestrator contract makes renaming expensive: keep `single_open`/`single_closed` as the current stacked pair and add a clearly named columns pair, or explicitly migrate to `stacked_*` only if the child/main compatibility cost is understood.
3. In the child orchestrator, model content arrangement as a separate axis from sidebar visibility. Do not make `toggle_sidebar` mean "next swap layout".
4. The columns variant should split only the non-sidebar content area. The managed `sidebar` pane identity and command stay unchanged.
5. If the shared child plugin cannot safely grow this contract without risking main Yazelix's current `yzx_side.swap.kdl`, stop and record the smaller child-plugin contract needed for runtime-specific layout sets.
6. Treat the content-layout key as a separate design choice. A future command/menu item is better than binding `Alt Shift h` to layout cycling.

## Verification

- `cargo test --manifest-path /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-zellij-pane-orchestrator/Cargo.toml --lib` should exit 0 if child orchestrator logic changes.
- `nix build /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-zellij-pane-orchestrator#yazelix_zellij_pane_orchestrator --no-link` should exit 0 if the child package changes.
- `nix flake check` should exit 0 and prove the packaged Nova layout/check contract.
- `nix build .#yazelix --no-link --print-build-logs` should exit 0 after Nova layout or lock changes.
- `nix profile add --refresh /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix --profile <tmp>` should install the package if runtime files change.
- `nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix` after runtime package changes.
- Manual dogfooding before claiming UX: toggle sidebar in both stacked and columns modes and confirm the Yazi sidebar remains the same managed pane.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if `Alt Shift h` is still raw `NextSwapLayout`.
- Stop if adding columns requires broad layout-family generation from main Yazelix.
- Stop if the shared orchestrator change would make main Yazelix cycle into swap names that main does not ship.
- Stop if the implementation starts defining bottom-terminal pane role/cwd/focus policy; create a separate bead only if the user chooses it.

## Maintenance Notes

Current decision: sidebar-aware content modes are swap variants, not real layouts. Rejected for this slice: bottom-terminal layout, main Yazelix's broader layout family system, and raw swap-cycle UX.

title: Evaluate remaining Starship integration improvements
description: Audit the open Starship request against Nova’s existing managed Starship config, live defaults, Nushell integration, and bar facts, then identify any remaining user-visible gap. Keep existing ownership intact and avoid a second prompt materializer.
acceptance_criteria: Inventory the behavior already delivered by Nova and the unmet request behind issue #640. Close as satisfied if no concrete gap remains; otherwise define one owner and one focused implementation bead with a user-visible verification path. Do not duplicate Starship configuration ownership.

title: Strengthen the Nova doctor recovery contract
description: Improve yzx doctor from Nova runtime incidents, keeping diagnosis read-only by default and adding repairs only when they are bounded, backup-first, reversible, and owned by one audited mutation path. Preserve stable structured output and actionable refusals. Do not port Classic doctor providers or build a generic repair framework before concrete recurring failures justify it.
acceptance_criteria: Audit current Nova doctor against real incidents and choose the smallest high-value additions. Every added detector has stable human and structured output plus an actionable next step. Any mutation is backup-first, reversible, idempotent, explicitly requested, and tested against its failure mode. No speculative generic recovery framework.

title: Delete cursor JSONC after the TOML cutover
description:
## Why

The TOML transition is incomplete while `yazelix-cursors` still carries JSONC parsing, text migrations, rendering, paths, documentation, dependencies, and tests. Keeping both after every consumer has moved would preserve two configuration owners and make future schema changes pay twice.

## Contract

After all published consumers use TOML and existing user state has had the agreed migration opportunity, the child recognizes only `cursors.toml` as cursor configuration. A leftover `settings.jsonc` is never silently preferred or merged. Consumer diagnostics point to the TOML path and the documented migration/recovery action.

## Evidence

- `src/lib.rs:274-414,975-1050` contains JSONC-only parsing, migration, persistence, and rendering.
- `src/bin/yzc.rs:17,215-249,556-585` hardcodes the JSONC filename and workflow.
- `Cargo.toml:18-23` includes JSONC/migration dependencies.
- README, flake help text, and shader documentation reference `settings.jsonc`.
- The parent epic requires main Yazelix, Nova, Mars, standalone `yzc`, and Ghostty to move before deletion.

## Scope

In scope:
- Delete the legacy JSONC loader/importer after the coordinated consumer release has landed.
- Delete JSONC-only renderer/migration helpers, tests, error variants/messages, constants, and dependencies.
- Remove stale JSONC references from package metadata, README, examples, shaders docs, and target contracts.
- Verify no main or Nova locked consumer still calls removed APIs before publishing.
- Record the final supported TOML paths per consumer.

Out of scope:
- Do not redesign the TOML schema.
- Do not retain an undocumented alias or dual-read fallback.
- Do not change cursor visuals, shaders, randomness, or terminal rendering.
- Do not start until the main Yazelix migration bead and `the completed staging work` are complete and published.

## Implementation Notes

1. Search all sibling released consumers before deletion.
2. Delete first; avoid replacement compatibility abstractions.
3. Dependency decision: remove `jsonc-parser` and the Ratconfig migration dependency if they have no remaining non-legacy use. Add no crates.
4. Keep only tests that defend TOML behavior or a real stale-file diagnostic.

## Verification

- `rg -n 'settings\\.jsonc|jsonc|load_cursor_settings_jsonc|render_cursor_settings_jsonc' . --glob '!target/**'` should return no live cursor-config surface.
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` and `cargo test` should exit 0.
- `nix build .#yazelix_cursors --no-link` should succeed.
- Locked main Yazelix and Yazelix Nova builds should succeed without local overrides.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if any released consumer still requires JSONC; update its migration bead rather than keeping ambiguous precedence.
- Stop if deletion would strand a Home Manager-owned file without a documented declarative TOML replacement.

## Maintenance Notes

Expected net LOC is negative, likely deleting 180 to 300 lines plus two dependencies. This bead is the explicit end of the compatibility window, not an indefinite P3 cleanup.
notes: Classic TOML migration and the Nova cursor bridge are complete.

title: Unify Yazelix cursors on TOML
description:
## Outcome

Make TOML the only persisted Yazelix Cursors configuration format while keeping one semantic owner. The cursor crate owns the registry schema, parsing, validation, custom definitions, appearance-aware random resolution, and terminal-neutral resolved state. Mars renders that state, Yazelix Nova exposes it through Ratconfig and passes the config path, and Ghostty consumes the same selection through child-owned generation.

## Architecture

- Yazelix Nova user config: `~/.config/yazelix/cursors.toml`.
- Standalone `yzc`: TOML config under its existing XDG-owned directory.
- Mars depends directly on the `yazelix-cursors` Rust crate and receives an explicit config path from `yzx`.
- Ratconfig renders controls and advanced-file access; it does not own cursor semantics or graphics.
- JSONC exists only as a temporary backup-first import path during the released consumer cutover and is deleted afterward.

## Program Slices

1. Publish the child-owned TOML load/render/migration contract.
2. Complete Mars bead `yzt-8tg` by replacing its mirrored presets with the child crate.
3. Complete the rewritten Yazelix Nova Mars/Ratconfig bridge bead.
4. Migrate main Yazelix and Home Manager through its sidecar-adoption epic.
5. Remove the JSONC parser, renderer, migrations, dependencies, and documentation after all released consumers use TOML.
6. Complete the existing P3 user-owned Ghostty integration against the shared TOML source.

## Rejected Alternatives

- Do not store cross-terminal cursor selection in `mars/config.toml`.
- Do not keep `~/.config/yazelix_cursors/settings.jsonc` as a permanent second owner.
- Do not materialize temporary Mars TOML at launch.
- Do not copy the cursor catalog or random-selection policy into Mars, Yazelix, or Yazelix Nova.
- Do not add file watching or live per-window synchronization in the first slice.

## Completion

The program is complete when Mars and Ghostty consume the child-owned cursor model, Ratconfig edits the relevant TOML source, canonical Yazelix no longer references cursor JSONC, and the child contains no JSONC cursor configuration compatibility code.
notes: Mars and Classic TOML migrations are complete. Remaining work is final JSONC deletion and the optional user-owned Ghostty integration.

title: Evaluate user-declared Nova Zellij layouts
description: Reevaluate the real user request for sidebar/editor/aux layout customization against Nova’s fixed, generated layout and slim Home Manager boundary. Decide among an explicit custom KDL source, a few curated layouts, a narrow profile surface, or no new surface. Do not import Classic layout-family machinery or invent a second layout language.
acceptance_criteria: The decision records the irreducible user workflow, evaluates custom KDL source versus curated layouts versus declarative profiles versus no new surface, and names the single layout owner. Any accepted option covers Home Manager source ownership and sidebar/editor/aux roles without duplicating Nova layout generation. Implementation beads exist only for an accepted design.

title: Evaluate a Beads viewer for the Yazelix website
description: Evaluate whether a read-only Beads-backed website view makes the public roadmap more useful without exposing maintainer-only planning or coupling the site to the local SQLite database. Prefer generated public data and the established publication boundary.
acceptance_criteria: Prototype or evaluate a read-only public dataset generated from publishable Beads fields, excluding internal-only issues and local database state. Record freshness, hosting, privacy, and maintenance cost. End with adopt/reject; if accepted, implementation belongs to the website repository and preserves one planning source of truth.

title: Define Nova support for Windows and WSL
description: Define and verify the honest Nova support boundary for WSL and native Windows. Start with Nix, terminal, Mars, Zellij, filesystem, and desktop constraints; document unsupported paths explicitly. Do not introduce Windows compatibility code or claim native support without a viable package/runtime owner.
acceptance_criteria: Record a capability matrix for WSL and native Windows covering installation, supported package manager/Nix path, yzx command floor, terminal entry, Mars availability, Zellij/Yazi/Helix behavior, filesystem semantics, and desktop integration. Verify the cheapest executable path available and document explicit unsupported behavior. Implementation requires a separately approved platform slice.

title: Add optional hide-on-file-open behavior for the managed Yazi sidebar
description:
## Why

Opening a file from the managed Yazi sidebar currently leaves the sidebar visible. Some users prefer the editor to take the recovered space automatically after a managed file open, while others rely on the sidebar staying visible for repeated navigation.

## Product direction

Evaluate and, if the interaction remains useful in dogfooding, add one Nova-native opt-in policy that hides the managed Yazi sidebar after a successful managed file open. This is a future Nova feature, not a Classic compatibility requirement and not a cutover blocker.

## Scope

- Define the exact event: only a successful managed Yazi-to-editor open may trigger the behavior.
- Route the action through the existing pane orchestrator instead of teaching Yazi layout geometry.
- Preserve current visible-sidebar behavior by default unless dogfooding supports changing the default.
- Decide the smallest honest Nova setting path, if a setting is justified.
- Keep Ctrl y and Alt Shift h behavior coherent when the sidebar was hidden automatically.
- Add focused behavior coverage and document the setting and apply timing.

## Guardrails

- Do not add a Classic field alias, migration reader, or compatibility table to Nova.
- Do not hide the sidebar when the editor open fails.
- Do not make the Yazi opener own pane geometry or layout state.
- Do not block the Classic-to-Nova source swap on this feature.

## Verification

- A successful managed file open hides the sidebar only when enabled.
- Disabled behavior leaves the sidebar visible.
- Failed opens preserve the current layout.
- Ctrl y can reopen and focus the hidden sidebar, then return focus to the editor.
- Existing reveal and sidebar toggle flows remain valid.

title: Document and validate Zellij session sharing in Nova
description: Verify ordinary attach and Zellij web sharing against a published Nova session, then document the smallest accurate browser, mobile, and pair-programming path plus security caveats. Add a Yazelix helper only if Nova creates demonstrated friction.
acceptance_criteria: Published Nova is tested with ordinary multi-client attach and Zellij web sharing where available. Documentation records exact commands, authentication and HTTPS caveats, managed-runtime expectations, and mobile/browser limitations. A helper is added only for demonstrated Nova-specific friction.

title: Define Nova support for native Zellij session switching
description: Define whether and how Nova supports Zellij native session switching, including the exact native command or documented escape hatch, managed layout/session identity effects, and interaction with the Herdr-inspired attach flow. Keep switching distinct from creating, attaching, sharing, or resurrecting sessions.
acceptance_criteria: The decision names the supported native command or explicit rejection, documents effects on managed session and bridge identity, and distinguishes switch, attach, fresh create, share, and resurrection. Any accepted surface has one focused command-contract check and a published-runtime manual smoke plan.

title: Recover canonical Nova lineage without replay
description: Forensic recovery from the prohibited closure of FlexNetOS PR #12, cherry-pick replay, rebase, and incomplete PR #21 merge. Preserve complete canonical and FlexNetOS history through an explicit unrelated-history merge with canonical 1bf5aa32 as first parent and FlexNetOS 4112c7ee as second parent. Final content must use Nova architecture including runtime/, preserve required FlexNetOS behavior without a Classic/Nova hybrid, keep nushell/scripts and nushell/config as built profile inputs, make Nushell mandatory with no non-Nushell fallback, preserve config/runtime/profile ownership, build before live profile mutation, and produce exactly one profile element and one desktop launcher/launch path. Verification requires source contracts, package build/store path, temporary profile proof, final review, manual test gate, live profile/frontdoor/generated runtime/Nushell/launcher proof, and clean PR/branch/worktree state. Rust dependency decision: production crates unchanged; dev-only crates unchanged; implement small recovery/domain logic with std and existing workspace crates; reject new convenience/framework crates; packaging impact is confined to the canonical flake/package and profile-owned desktop/runtime outputs.
notes: Forensics proved PR #12 head 83ee66121471ce202749da207aef880030d13ad0 had four commits and closed unmerged; PR #21 merge 2beee6dca0ca3436aa1de00647921e4ce831d5ad retained only three replayed commits and omitted 5ee454b8 Plan yzn-shaped config simplification; .git/logs/HEAD records the prohibited cherry-pick/rebase replay.
